The tools did include multiple attachments which was a nice feature but the quality of those attachments was poor, the clamp on the straightener attachment was pretty loose and kept falling off at the worst times. The price point for the set was also a bit high considering the quality of the tools was average at best. I ended up returning the straightener as it just wasn't worth the hassle of dealing with its problems but the curling iron has been kept out of frustration I guess.

I recently tried the new tropical sunscreen that everyone was raving about but unfortunately it was a huge letdown. The first time I applied it, it felt smooth and pleasant on my skin but that was where the positives ended. Less than an hour later I could already start to feel it breaking down and losing its effectiveness, even after reapplying as instructed. When I checked my progress with a spray tan machine in a store, I was shocked to see how uneven and patchy my coverage was, with large areas completely lacking protection. Additionally, the smell it left on my skin was overpowering and lingered long after I showered. For the price I paid, I expected a lot better performance and coverage from a product that claims to be top of the line. Needless to say, I will not be purchasing this product again.

I recently tried a series of face masks that left me somewhat uninspired. The initial excitement of opening a new sheet mask was often short-lived as they failed to deliver on their claims of hydrating and brightening my skin. Upon application, some of them felt too sticky or tight, making it uncomfortable to wear for the recommended 15-20 minutes.  The scents were overpowering and lingered long after I removed the mask, sticking to my skin and clothing. Some of the formulas contained ingredients I'm sensitive to, causing redness and irritation. Although I experienced some minor improvements in skin texture, which I suppose could be due to the occasional decent ingredients included, it was overshadowed by the overwhelming negatives. Overall, I felt like I didn't get what I paid for, especially considering the variety of natural and organic options available that promise better results.

I recently purchased the natural deodorant by Green Earth, and I have to say it's been a bit of a disappointment. The scent is not as strong as I expected, and it wears off after a few hours. I also noticed that I sweat more than usual, which is not ideal for someone who's already prone to excessive sweating. The texture is decent, it applies smoothly and doesn't leave any residue behind, that's a plus. But the effectiveness just doesn't live up to the claims made by the manufacturer. I've had to reapply it multiple times a day, which is inconvenient. I also noticed that it contains artificial fragrances, which is something I specifically avoided when making my purchase. This is a major letdown, because I thought I was buying a natural product. Overall, I would not recommend this deodorant to anyone looking for a reliable and long-lasting solution.

I recently purchased this hair straightener and I have mixed feelings about it. 
The design looks sleek and modern which I appreciated, however when I started using it, I experienced some issues. 
The plates are made of ceramic which is supposed to be good for my hair but it didn't make a significant difference compared to other straighteners I've used. 
It does get very hot which I liked, but I had to be extremely cautious not to burn my hair. 
I also noticed that it took a bit longer to straighten my hair than I expected, which was frustrating. 
Additionally, the temperature control wasn't as precise as I had hoped it would be, so I had to constantly switch between different heat settings to get the right temperature for my hair. 
Overall I think it's an okay product but it could be improved upon, that's why I'm giving it a 3 out of 5 stars.

I used the small, black five star g-whiz twice in 48 hours. I rinsed the blades off with tap water. Next morning, the blades had rust on them. I got a full refund by Amazon since I bought it directly from them. End of story.

Wahl customer support, I want to buy and like your products, but now I've got to ask which of your products do not have this problem. I just want a cordless battery powered trimmer that won't rust. As sales and business owners, it really doesn't make economic sense to sell something with metal in it guaranteed to rust as soon as it hits water, since people are going to do a 180 and get a refund.

Maybe the metal does have the benefit of being easier to machine and result in higher quality blades, but it's really not an option to have easily oxidized metal in a product that gets washed frequently.

I'm open to buying Wahl trimmers again, but I need recommendations for which models won't have fundamental engineering design flaws. I'll spend the money, just tell me what actually works.

P.S. the trimmer works fine otherwise. Maybe a little wimpy in terms of power output, but no hair pulling. Pretty noisy, but who cares. Just lubricate it properly. It's smaller than the Remington MB4900, but the blade width is almost as long. It's lightweight and very small, so I guess if you have gigantic man-hands it might not offer the best ergonomics. I actually prefer non-permanent batteries, so that was a plus for me. The trimmer heads aren't easily removable for cleaning. I don't think the body is very waterproof (aside from the obvious aforementioned blade rusting), so be wary about using it in the shower.

Unfortunately, these brushes only added to the frustration of trying to do my makeup. They're definitely not worth the money and I would not recommend them to anyone.

Brush + Case + Sponge:
The Kabuki brush is a little bit long for my taste for being a travel size brush, but it is still much smaller than a lot of other travel size kabuki brushes on the market. The case is cute but a little bit heavy. I hope in the future the company will consider making it lighter and thus even more portable. Some people might like the substantial feel though. The sponge (is it a sponge?) that came with the case is pretty confusing, as it is not a conventional size nor texture for these sorts of makeup. I don't use it as intended. Instead, I placed it directly on top of the foundation to serve as a cushion when I'm moving around to prevent the foundation from shattering.

Foundation:
The foundation itself is pretty great. It doesn't feel grainy on skin and helps even out my complexion without drying my skin. I have light to medium Asian skin tone, and the shade Supernatural works just fine. It is slightly lighter than my skin tone when I'm tanned, but I only apply it around the center of my face anyways, so there is less concern about blending to the neck to begin with. I might try a shade darker if I get tanner in the future.

I use a brush to apply the foundation and have it settle into my pores before I apply another layer on top with my own sponge (purchased from Muji) to give more coverage if needed. When I'm on the go, skipping the brush step is usually fine. I can sort of squeeze my sponge into the designated slot.

The service was also top-notch, with fast shipping and a friendly and helpful customer service team. I highly recommend this product to anyone looking to add some extra oomph to their style. Five stars is not enough - this product deserves ten stars if possible.
I got a lot of complements on the color.

I've had mixed feelings about this hair oil/serum and I'm still unsure if it's worth the hype or not. On the one hand, my hair does feel slightly softer and more manageable after using it, but I don't think it's as effective as some other products I've tried in the past. The packaging is also a bit annoying because the dropper is really sensitive and it's easy to overdo it, which results in a greasy mess. Overall, I'd say this product is okay but not great. I wouldn't recommend it to anyone with super oily hair because it might just make things worse.

i recently tried these nail polish and press-on nails and was moderately impressed. the colors were vibrant and the formulas were smooth, making them easy to apply. however, the wear time wasn't as long as i had hoped it would be, and i had to touch up the polish a few times throughout the week. the press-on nails themselves were a bit tricky to put on and didn't fit as well as i would have liked, especially around my cuticles. they looked nice at first but started to lift and chip after a few days. overall, i would say these are an ok product, but definitely not the best i've seen. i would use them again in a pinch, but i wouldn't go out of my way to recommend them to others.

I am thoroughly impressed with this sunscreen - it offers excellent protection against UV damage, has a lightweight and non-greasy texture that makes it perfect for daily use, and is easy to apply and reapply throughout the day.
If you want someone to know you are wearing a wig ... then buy it. I returned because hair was “Barbie doll shiny hair”, lol. Not my taste. Beautiful style just to plastic shiny looking for me.

I recently purchased the X-Treme Hair Straightener and I have to say it's been a game changer for my hair care routine. The sleek design and sturdy build of the straightener make it feel premium and well-made, and the ergonomic handle is comfortable to hold even after extended use. The straightener itself is easy to use with the simple temperature controls and the ionic technology really helps to reduce frizz and enhance shine. I've been using it on a medium heat setting for my daily styling and it's left my hair looking smooth and salon-quality. One of the things that really stands out about this straightener is how quick and easy it is to use. The heat up time is almost instant and the ceramic plates are a breeze to clean after use. I've also been impressed with how well it performs on thick or curly hair, it glides through with ease and doesn't get hot spots like some other straighteners I've used. I recently purchased the beard and mustache grooming kit and I'm overall satisfied with the results, although it falls short in a few areas. The kit comes with a good variety of tools including a comb, scissors, balm and conditioner, making it a convenient bundle. The conditioner works well to detangle and soften my beard, but some of the balm and conditioner seems to be running a bit low even after light usage.  The scissors cut well but require a bit more force than I'm used to which can be difficult with longer beard hairs. However the kit is well-made, compact and the price was quite reasonable. One thing that might be a slight drawback for some customers is that the kit does not come with any tutorials or online resources which might be helpful for beginners or those looking to achieve specific styles.
